credential_nomenclature: Registered Dietitian (RD/RDN) — No State License Required

seo.title: Arizona Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer dietitian license to arizona, dietitian compact arizona, arizona dietitian endorsement application

board.name: No Dedicated State Licensing Board
board.parent_dept: Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: No state application fee. CDR exam fee is $200 nationally.
quick_facts.renewal_cycle: No state renewal. CDR registration renews every 5 years with 75 CPE hours.

license_types.title: Registered Dietitian / Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RD/RDN)
license_types.description: Arizona does not issue a state-level dietitian license. Instead, practicing dietitians use the national Registered Dietitian (RD) or Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) credential issued by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Complete Required Education
steps.description: Complete a didactic program in dietetics and a supervised practice program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination
steps.description: Pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Maintain Registration
steps.description: Maintain registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) by completing 75 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) every 5 years.

documents.detail: Your active CDR card serves as your primary credential in Arizona.
Proof of completion of your accredited degree program and supervised practice.

provisional_text: Because Arizona does not require a state license, there is no provisional or temporary state license available.
reciprocity_text: Arizona has no state licensing requirements to transfer. Your national CDR credential (RD/RDN) is recognized directly. No state endorsement process exists.

faqs.question: Does Arizona require a state dietitian license?
faqs.answer: No. Arizona does not mandate state-specific licensure for dietitians. Your national CDR registration as a Registered Dietitian (RD) or Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) is the recognized credential.

faqs.question: What does it cost to practice as a dietitian in Arizona?
faqs.answer: There is no state application or renewal fee. The CDR national exam costs $200, and the annual CDR maintenance fee is $80 (increasing to $90 in 2027).

faqs.question: Is there pending legislation to require state licensure in Arizona?
faqs.answer: As of 2026, Arizona does not have an active, enacted state licensure law for dietitians. Legislative efforts (such as H.B. 2820 from 2021) have been introduced in the past.

faqs.question: Does Arizona have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Arizona is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Arizona?
faqs.answer: This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.

reciprocity.notes: Arizona has no state licensing requirements to transfer. Your national CDR credential (RD/RDN) is recognized directly. No state endorsement process exists.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Arizona
hero.dek: Arizona does not currently require state licensure for dietitians or nutritionists. As such, there is no state licensing board or official application portal for state licensure in Arizona.

official_links.registration_steps: Arizona does not require state licensure for dietitians or nutritionists. Therefore, there is no state application portal or registration process for state licensure.
